title: Agentic Engineering Weekly
subject: Agentic Engineering Weekly -- Week of 2026-03-23
summary: Claude Code adoption surpassed GitHub Copilot in Pragmatic Engineer's 2026 survey; 75% of engineers now use AI for half or more of their work
Multi-agent coordination emerged as the defining pattern: one agent codes, another tests, a third reviews
Context engineering (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md) shown to reduce agent runtime by 29%

stories.headline: What Top Engineers Are Actually Running
bullets: Standard stack converging on Cursor for daily IDE work + Claude Code for hard problems (architecture, multi-file refactors, debugging)
Codex CLI gaining traction for cost-sensitive autonomous tasks; uses ~3x fewer tokens but scores lower on SWE-bench (64.7% vs 80.8%)
Devin shipping PRs autonomously at Goldman Sachs and Nubank; best for well-defined migrations and test writing at $500/month

stories.headline: Kiro: Amazon's Spec-Driven IDE
bullets: New IDE from AWS that generates requirements docs, design specs, and task breakdowns before writing code
Powered by Claude Sonnet; limited to 50 interactions/month on free tier
Best suited for teams building complex backend systems where upfront planning pays off

stories.headline: Multi-Agent Workflows Go Mainstream
bullets: Anthropic's 2026 trends report identifies single-to-multi-agent shift as the year's defining pattern
Typical setup: one agent per feature, one for tests, one for review, all coordinated via shared context files
Task horizons expanding from minutes to days/weeks with agents maintaining coherent state across sessions

stories.headline: Context Engineering Is the Highest-Leverage Skill
bullets: Martin Fowler published detailed guide on context engineering for coding agents
AGENTS.md files reduce median agent runtime by 29% per Anthropic research
Key elements: path-based rules, subagent delegation, lifecycle hooks, skill systems

stories.headline: Notable Launches This Month
bullets: Codex CLI hit 67K GitHub stars; OpenAI positioning it as the open-source alternative to Claude Code
Claude Code added worktree isolation and background agents for parallel development
GitHub Copilot shipped agent mode to free tier users

what_to_watch: Whether multi-agent setups converge on a standard coordination protocol or stay tool-specific
Devin's expansion beyond migrations into greenfield feature work
Claude Code's roadmap for persistent agent memory across sessions
